findings.title: Hardcoded 2FA test OTP '123456' grants Super Admin access
findings.description: A hardcoded test OTP '123456' bypasses all 2FA checks when APP_ENV != 'production'. Since the deployed environment runs as APP_ENV=local, any attacker can authenticate as Super Admin by calling verify-2fa with otp=123456, receiving a bearer token with full ['*'] permissions.
findings.evidence: curl -X POST /api/v1/auth/verify-2fa -d '{"email":"admin@test.com","otp":"123456"}' -> 200 OK with token '2|uutE3j79W7iLAU3fpj8sd0BycUr8CiAvH8KuOiIR188d9b8b', role: admin.super, permissions: ["*"]. Code: $isTestOtp = !app()->environment('production') && $request->otp === '123456';
findings.impact: Complete system takeover. Attacker gains Super Admin access to all admin functions, user management, data export, settings, and complaint data.
findings.remediation: Remove the hardcoded test OTP bypass entirely. If needed for development, protect behind APP_TESTING_2FA_BYPASS=true flag that is never set outside local dev machines.

findings.title: Live API keys (Gemini, OpenAI) stored in plaintext .env files on server
findings.description: Real, active API keys for Google Gemini (AIzaSyDt5...) and OpenAI (sk-proj-qKVos...) are stored in plaintext in ai-service/.env. The root .env also contains commented-out but complete API keys. If the server is compromised, these keys grant immediate access to paid AI services.
findings.evidence: ai-service/.env: GEMINI_API_KEY=AIzaSyDt5adSNhsq5pSNLgIQ1KZvgb8oBaX6M-I, OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-proj-qKVosGEd9QEyCk0wc5oFSy5oz-...
findings.impact: Unauthorized AI API usage, financial costs, potential data exposure through AI providers.
findings.remediation: Rotate all exposed API keys immediately. Use Docker secrets or a vault solution for production. Never store real keys in files on shared servers.

findings.title: Default database credentials 'surian_user:surian_pass' with externally exposed PostgreSQL
findings.description: PostgreSQL uses trivially guessable default credentials hardcoded across all environments. Port 5432 is exposed externally, allowing direct database access from the internet using these known credentials.
findings.evidence: docker-compose.yml: POSTGRES_USER=surian_user, POSTGRES_PASSWORD=surian_pass. Port mapping: 5432:5432.
findings.impact: Complete database compromise. Attacker can read/modify/delete all data including user records, complaints, PII.
findings.remediation: Generate strong random password. Remove port 5432 external exposure. Use Docker secrets for credential injection.

findings.title: Plaintext OTPs and password reset tokens logged to application logs
findings.description: OTP codes and password reset URLs (including tokens) are logged in plaintext via Log::info. Anyone with log access can read OTPs to bypass 2FA or use reset tokens to take over any account.
findings.evidence: Log::info("OTP for user {$user->email}: {$otp}"); Log::info("Password reset link for {$user->email}: {$resetUrl}");
findings.impact: 2FA bypass and account takeover for any user via log file access.
findings.remediation: Remove sensitive values from log messages. Log only that an OTP was sent or reset was requested, without the actual codes/tokens.

findings.title: APP_KEY, DB password, and SMTP credentials committed in backend/.env
findings.description: The backend .env file contains hardcoded APP_KEY, database credentials, and Gmail SMTP app password. These secrets are exposed to anyone with repository or server access.
findings.evidence: APP_KEY=base64:uaCen/K1wMZ7HYQAxXdQRWl1AdGeb556vVnL+ZaOP1I=, DB_PASSWORD=surian_pass, MAIL_PASSWORD="njdd cdff iejb psbs"
findings.impact: Application decryption key compromise, database access, email spoofing as ministry.
findings.remediation: Rotate APP_KEY, DB password, and Gmail app password. Remove .env from repository. Use environment-level config or secrets manager.

findings.title: AI service accepts user-controlled system_prompt parameter on public endpoints
findings.description: The ChatRequest, SummarizeRequest, and TranslateRequest models accept an optional system_prompt parameter. The AI service is exposed on port 8001 without authentication. Any caller can override the system prompt to control AI behavior, extract internal information, or generate harmful content.
findings.evidence: class ChatRequest(BaseModel): prompt: str; system_prompt: Optional[str] = None. In chat endpoint: system = request.system_prompt if request.system_prompt else settings.CHAT_SYSTEM_PROMPT
findings.impact: Complete AI behavior override. Attacker can make the AI reveal config, generate harmful content, or produce disinformation as the ministry.
findings.remediation: Remove system_prompt from all public request models. Use server-side system prompts only. Restrict AI service to internal network access.

findings.title: Backup export endpoint allows arbitrary table data extraction without allowlist
findings.description: The export endpoint accepts a user-controlled 'tables' parameter and dumps entire table contents. An authenticated admin can export any database table including users (password hashes, OTPs), sessions, personal_access_tokens.
findings.evidence: $tables = $request->input('tables', ['users', 'complaints', ...]); foreach ($tables as $table) { $data['tables'][$table] = DB::table($table)->get()->toArray(); }
findings.impact: Full database extraction including password hashes, session tokens, and PII.
findings.remediation: Implement strict allowlist of exportable tables. Filter sensitive columns from any exported data.

findings.title: Admin dashboard routes lack RBAC — any authenticated user can access admin functions
findings.description: All admin routes are protected only by 'auth' middleware without role-based checks. Any authenticated user (including citizens) can access user management, content management, roles/permissions if they can reach the admin IP range.
findings.evidence: Route::middleware('auth')->group(function () { Route::prefix('users')... Route::prefix('roles')... });
findings.impact: Privilege escalation from citizen to admin capabilities.
findings.remediation: Add role middleware: Route::middleware(['auth', 'role:admin.*'])->group(...).

findings.title: Complaint tracking endpoint exposes PII fields without authentication
findings.description: The complaint tracking endpoint returns all database columns including national_id, phone, email, user_id, AI confidence scores, and file paths. Only the tracking number is required — no identity verification.
findings.evidence: Response includes: national_id, phone, email, user_id, ai_confidence, ai_suggested_directorate_id, file_path fields. Tracking numbers follow predictable pattern CMP-YYYYMMDD-XXXXX.
findings.impact: PII exposure for any citizen who filed a complaint. Tracking numbers can be enumerated.
findings.remediation: Apply JSON resource/transformer to return only safe fields. Require secondary verification (phone/email match) before showing details.

findings.title: CORS allows any .gov.sy subdomain with credentials
findings.description: The CORS pattern '/^https?:\/\/.*\.gov\.sy$/' with supports_credentials:true allows any .gov.sy subdomain to make authenticated cross-origin requests. A compromised government subdomain could steal session data.
findings.evidence: curl -H 'Origin: https://evil.gov.sy' -> Access-Control-Allow-Origin: https://evil.gov.sy, Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true
findings.impact: Cross-origin session hijacking from any compromised .gov.sy subdomain.
findings.remediation: Restrict to specific known subdomains. Remove HTTP allowance (HTTPS only).

findings.title: Missing rate limiting on forgot-password and verify-2fa endpoints
findings.description: The forgot-password and verify-2fa endpoints have no throttle middleware. Forgot-password enables email bombing; verify-2fa enables OTP brute force (1M combinations for 6-digit OTP).
findings.evidence: Route::post('verify-2fa', ...) and Route::post('forgot-password', ...) with no ->middleware('throttle:...')
findings.impact: 2FA brute force, email bombing, account takeover.
findings.remediation: Add throttle middleware: ->middleware('throttle:5,15') for verify-2fa, ->middleware('throttle:3,15') for forgot-password.

findings.title: Sanctum tokens never expire
findings.description: Token expiration is set to null, meaning API tokens never expire. A stolen token remains valid indefinitely.
findings.evidence: 'expiration' => null,
findings.impact: Indefinite access window for stolen tokens.
findings.remediation: Set expiration to env('SANCTUM_TOKEN_EXPIRATION', 1440) (24 hours).

findings.title: Auth tokens stored in localStorage — vulnerable to XSS exfiltration
findings.description: Bearer tokens stored in localStorage across 60+ locations. Any XSS vulnerability allows token theft.
findings.evidence: localStorage.getItem('auth_token') used in 60+ files including repository.ts, api.ts, dashboard pages, admin pages.
findings.impact: Token theft via any XSS vector, leading to account takeover.
findings.remediation: Migrate to httpOnly cookie-based auth via Sanctum SPA authentication.

findings.title: dangerouslySetInnerHTML renders unsanitized CMS content (stored XSS)
findings.description: CMS content rendered via dangerouslySetInnerHTML without DOMPurify sanitization. Compromised CMS admin or injected content enables stored XSS against all visitors.
findings.evidence: dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: detailModal.decree.content_ar }}
findings.impact: Stored XSS affecting all site visitors viewing decree content.
findings.remediation: Sanitize with DOMPurify: dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: DOMPurify.sanitize(content) }}.

findings.title: No SSL/TLS — all traffic over plain HTTP on public IP
findings.description: Nginx only listens on port 80. All traffic including auth cookies, tokens, and PII traverses unencrypted on public IP 91.230.110.187.
findings.evidence: listen 80; — No listen 443 ssl, no ssl_certificate directives.
findings.impact: All credentials and data interceptable via network sniffing.
findings.remediation: Configure TLS with Let's Encrypt. Redirect HTTP to HTTPS. Add HSTS header.

findings.title: All AI endpoints completely unauthenticated
findings.description: All 10 AI service endpoints (chat, analyze-complaint, summarize, suggest-title, proofread, ocr, embeddings, translate, health, providers) require no authentication. Anyone on the network can call them directly.
findings.evidence: @app.post("/api/v1/ai/chat") async def chat(request: ChatRequest, provider: AIProvider = Depends(get_ai_provider)): — No auth dependency on any endpoint.
findings.impact: Unauthorized AI API consumption, prompt injection, cost abuse.
findings.remediation: Add API key validation via X-API-Key header using a FastAPI dependency. Restrict network access to backend container only.

findings.title: Redis running without authentication
findings.description: Redis started without --requirepass. REDIS_PASSWORD=null. Any container on the Docker network can access Redis cache/session data.
findings.evidence: docker-compose.yml: redis service has no --requirepass. backend/.env: REDIS_PASSWORD=null.
findings.impact: Cache poisoning, session data theft.
findings.remediation: Add --requirepass to Redis command. Set REDIS_PASSWORD in backend config.

findings.title: APP_DEBUG=true on public-facing server exposes stack traces
findings.description: Debug mode enabled in Laravel. 500 errors return 263KB HTML pages with vendor paths, file names, line numbers, and full stack traces to any user.
findings.evidence: APP_DEBUG=true. Confirmed via DAST: POST /api/v1/complaints returned 500 with full debug page.
findings.impact: Internal architecture disclosure aids targeted attacks.
findings.remediation: Set APP_DEBUG=false and APP_ENV=production in all deployed environments.

findings.title: Sensitive file uploads stored in publicly accessible directory
findings.description: Investment application attachments, media uploads, and content attachments stored on 'public' disk. Anyone who knows/guesses the file path can access sensitive documents without authentication.
findings.evidence: $path = $file->store('investment-applications/' . $application->id, 'public');
findings.impact: Unauthorized access to investment proposals, complaint attachments, and other sensitive documents.
findings.remediation: Store sensitive uploads on 'local' disk. Serve through authenticated controller endpoints.

findings.title: PHP execution not blocked in upload directories
findings.description: Nginx does not restrict PHP execution in storage/uploads directories. If an attacker uploads a .php file, it could be executed by PHP-FPM.
findings.evidence: The location ~ \.php$ block matches any .php file under the document root without excluding storage/uploads paths.
findings.impact: Remote code execution via uploaded PHP files.
findings.remediation: Add: location ~* /storage/.*\.php$ { deny all; }

findings.title: No input sanitization or prompt injection filtering on AI user messages
findings.description: User input is passed directly to AI models with zero sanitization across all endpoints. No filtering for injection patterns, role-switching markers, or prompt extraction attempts.
findings.evidence: messages = [Message(role="user", content=request.prompt)] — direct passthrough.
findings.impact: Prompt injection enabling AI behavior manipulation, data extraction, harmful content generation.
findings.remediation: Implement input sanitization middleware. Use structured prompting with clear delimiters. Add output validation.

findings.title: OCR file upload has no type, size, or dimension validation
findings.description: The OCR endpoint reads the entire uploaded file into memory without any validation of file size, type, or dimensions. Client-provided content_type is trusted.
findings.evidence: content = await file.read() — no size limit. mime_type = file.content_type or 'image/png' — trusts client.
findings.impact: Memory exhaustion via large files, polyglot file attacks, pixel bombs.
findings.remediation: Validate: max 10MB size, allowlist mime types, verify magic bytes, limit dimensions to 10000x10000.

findings.title: AI service error responses leak internal exception details
findings.description: All AI endpoints return raw str(e) exception messages which may contain API keys, internal URLs, or stack traces.
findings.evidence: raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=str(e)) — used in chat, embedding, translation error handlers.
findings.impact: API key leakage, internal architecture disclosure.
findings.remediation: Return generic error messages. Log full exceptions server-side only.

findings.title: AI rate limiting configured but never implemented
findings.description: Rate limit settings exist in config (RATE_LIMIT_REQUESTS=100, RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW=60) but no rate limiting middleware is applied. No slowapi or fastapi-limiter installed.
findings.evidence: Settings defined but never consumed. No @limiter decorators on any endpoint.
findings.impact: Unlimited AI API consumption, cost abuse, denial of service.
findings.remediation: Install slowapi. Apply @limiter.limit('100/minute') to each endpoint.

findings.title: Backend and AI service containers run as root
findings.description: Neither Dockerfile includes a USER directive. Container processes run as root, expanding blast radius if compromised.
findings.evidence: No USER directive in backend/Dockerfile or ai-service/Dockerfile.
findings.impact: Container escape or privilege escalation if application is compromised.
findings.remediation: Add USER www-data (backend) and create a non-root appuser (AI service).

findings.title: Chat message endpoint lacks rate limiting — enables AI cost abuse
findings.description: The public chat/message endpoint has no throttle middleware, allowing unlimited AI requests and associated API costs.
findings.evidence: Route::post('message', [...ChatController::class, 'sendMessage']) — no throttle middleware.
findings.impact: Denial of service via AI API cost exhaustion.
findings.remediation: Add ->middleware('throttle:10,1') to chat/message endpoint.

findings.title: Webhook endpoints accept unsigned payloads and crash with 500
findings.description: WhatsApp and Telegram webhook endpoints accept payloads without signature validation and crash with 500 + full stack traces.
findings.evidence: POST /api/v1/webhooks/whatsapp/ with fake payload -> 500 with debug stack trace. No X-Hub-Signature-256 validation.
findings.impact: Webhook spoofing, information disclosure via error pages.
findings.remediation: Validate X-Hub-Signature-256 (WhatsApp) and secret token (Telegram) before processing. Return 401 for invalid signatures.

findings.title: No input length limits on AI endpoint request models
findings.description: ChatRequest, SummarizeRequest, TranslateRequest, EmbeddingRequest have no max_length constraints on text fields. Well-validated route modules exist but are dead code (never mounted).
findings.evidence: class ChatRequest(BaseModel): prompt: str — no Field(max_length=...). Route modules with proper validation exist but app.include_router() is never called.
findings.impact: Token exhaustion, memory pressure, excessive AI API costs.
findings.remediation: Add Field constraints or mount the existing well-validated route modules.

findings.title: CORS allows any port on public IP with credentials
findings.description: Origin pattern allows HTTP and HTTPS on any port for IP 91.230.110.187 with supports_credentials:true.
findings.evidence: '/^https?:\/\/91\.230\.110\.187(:\d+)?$/' with supports_credentials: true.
findings.impact: Any service on any port of the server can make authenticated cross-origin requests.
findings.remediation: Restrict to specific known ports.

findings.title: Broad CSRF token validation exemptions for user-facing API routes
findings.description: CSRF validation exempted for complaints/*, chat/*, suggestions/*, webhooks/*, and contact endpoints.
findings.evidence: validateCsrfTokens(except: ['api/v1/auth/*', 'api/v1/complaints/*', 'api/v1/chat/*', 'api/v1/suggestions/*', ...])
findings.impact: CSRF attacks on complaint and suggestion submission endpoints.
findings.remediation: Minimize exemptions. Ensure Sanctum SPA auth validates XSRF-TOKEN cookie.

findings.title: Nginx allows 512MB uploads — DoS vector
findings.description: client_max_body_size 512M combined with PHP upload_max_filesize=512M enables resource exhaustion.
findings.evidence: nginx.conf: client_max_body_size 512M; Dockerfile: upload_max_filesize=512M post_max_size=512M memory_limit=512M.
findings.impact: Server resource exhaustion via large file uploads.
findings.remediation: Reduce to 50-100MB. Apply per-endpoint limits if larger sizes needed.

findings.title: No memory/CPU limits on any Docker container
findings.description: No container has mem_limit, cpus, or deploy.resources configured. A runaway process can consume all host resources.
findings.evidence: No mem_limit, cpus, or deploy.resources in docker-compose.yml or docker-compose.dev.yml.
findings.impact: Single container can starve all other services.
findings.remediation: Add deploy.resources.limits for each service.

findings.title: All containers share single default Docker network — no segmentation
findings.description: No custom networks defined. Frontend can directly reach database and Redis.
findings.evidence: No 'networks:' key in docker-compose.yml.
findings.impact: Lateral movement if any container is compromised.
findings.remediation: Define separate networks: frontend-net, backend-net, data-net with appropriate service assignments.

findings.title: Session encryption disabled and secure cookie flag off
findings.description: SESSION_ENCRYPT=false, SESSION_SECURE_COOKIE=false, SESSION_LIFETIME=120 (vs recommended 15 minutes).
findings.evidence: backend/.env: SESSION_ENCRYPT=false, SESSION_SECURE_COOKIE=false, SESSION_LIFETIME=120.
findings.impact: Session data readable in transit. Cookies sent over HTTP.
findings.remediation: Set SESSION_ENCRYPT=true, SESSION_SECURE_COOKIE=true, reduce SESSION_LIFETIME to 15.

findings.title: No rate limiting at Nginx reverse proxy level
findings.description: While Laravel has API throttling (300/min), Nginx has no rate limiting. Attackers can overwhelm PHP-FPM before Laravel's limiter engages.
findings.evidence: No limit_req_zone or limit_req directives in nginx.conf.
findings.impact: Application-layer DoS bypassing Laravel rate limits.
findings.remediation: Add limit_req_zone and limit_req directives in nginx.conf.

findings.title: No restricted path protection in Nginx
findings.description: Nginx does not block access to .env, .git, composer.json, storage/ paths.
findings.evidence: Only two location blocks: ~ \.php$ and /. No deny rules for sensitive paths.
findings.impact: Potential exposure of configuration files if placed in public directory.
findings.remediation: Add: location ~ /\. { deny all; } and location ~ /(composer|artisan|storage) { deny all; }

findings.title: AI responses returned without output sanitization — potential XSS via AI
findings.description: AI responses returned verbatim to clients. If AI is tricked into outputting HTML/JS, it passes through unsanitized.
findings.evidence: return {"response": response.content, ...} — raw AI output.
findings.impact: XSS via AI-generated malicious content.
findings.remediation: Sanitize AI outputs. Frontend should treat AI responses as untrusted.

findings.title: AI provider selection overridable via query parameter by any user
findings.description: Any unauthenticated user can switch AI providers via ?provider=openai, consuming quota on potentially more expensive providers.
findings.evidence: def get_ai_provider(provider: Optional[str] = Query(None, ...)): return get_provider(provider)
findings.impact: Cost manipulation by switching to expensive providers.
findings.remediation: Remove provider override from production or gate behind admin auth.

findings.title: exec() used for database backup and virus scanning (mitigated with escapeshellarg)
findings.description: exec() used for pg_dump commands. Arguments properly escaped with escapeshellarg() but pattern is inherently risky.
findings.evidence: exec($command, $output, $returnCode); — command built with escapeshellarg().
findings.impact: Low risk due to escapeshellarg() but potential for future regression.
findings.remediation: Consider using Laravel's Process::run() for better safety.

findings.title: Backend controllers return raw exception messages in API responses
findings.description: Several controllers return $e->getMessage() in JSON error responses, leaking internal details.
findings.evidence: return response()->json(['error' => $e->getMessage()], 500);
findings.impact: Internal architecture disclosure.
findings.remediation: Log exceptions server-side, return generic error messages to clients.

findings.title: Investment application tracking requires only tracking number — no identity verification
findings.description: Investment application tracking endpoint is public, requiring only a tracking number to view personal and company details.
findings.evidence: Route::get('track/{trackingNumber}', [InvestmentApplicationController::class, 'track']);
findings.impact: PII exposure for investment applicants.
findings.remediation: Require secondary verification (national_id match). Add rate limiting.

findings.title: Frontend .dockerignore does not exclude .env file
findings.description: The .dockerignore excludes .env.local variants but not the base .env file. A .env file would be copied into the Docker image.
findings.evidence: .dockerignore lists .env.local, .env.development.local etc. but NOT .env.
findings.impact: Secrets potentially baked into Docker images.
findings.remediation: Add '.env' to frontend-next/.dockerignore.

findings.title: Vulnerable dependencies: python-multipart 0.0.6 (CVE-2024-24762), Pillow 10.2.0
findings.description: python-multipart 0.0.6 has ReDoS vulnerability. Pillow 10.2.0 has multiple known CVEs fixed in 10.3+. google-genai>=1.0.0 is unpinned.
findings.evidence: python-multipart==0.0.6, Pillow==10.2.0, google-genai>=1.0.0.
findings.impact: ReDoS, buffer overflow, supply chain risk from unpinned dependency.
findings.remediation: Update: python-multipart>=0.0.7, Pillow>=10.4.0. Pin google-genai to specific version.

findings.title: Well-validated AI route modules exist but are dead code — never mounted
findings.description: Route modules with proper Pydantic Field validations (max_length, ge, le constraints) exist but main.py defines its own inline models WITHOUT these validations. app.include_router() is never called.
findings.evidence: routes/chat.py has Field(..., max_length=4000) but main.py ChatRequest has no constraints. No include_router() calls.
findings.impact: Security validations exist in code but are not active.
findings.remediation: Mount route modules via app.include_router() and remove duplicate inline endpoints.

findings.title: Server version disclosure: nginx/1.29.4 and PHP/8.3.30
findings.description: Response headers reveal exact nginx and PHP versions.
findings.evidence: Server: nginx/1.29.4, X-Powered-By: PHP/8.3.30.
findings.impact: Aids version-specific exploit targeting.
findings.remediation: Add server_tokens off; in nginx. Set expose_php = Off in php.ini.

findings.title: Docker images use floating tags (:latest, :alpine)
findings.description: nginx:alpine, redis:7-alpine, node:20-alpine use floating tags that can change unexpectedly.
findings.evidence: image: nginx:alpine, image: redis:7-alpine, image: node:20-alpine.
findings.impact: Potential breaking changes or vulnerabilities from unexpected updates.
findings.remediation: Pin to specific versions: nginx:1.27-alpine, redis:7.4-alpine.

findings.title: Nginx server_tokens and autoindex not explicitly disabled
findings.description: server_tokens off and autoindex off not explicitly set in nginx config.
findings.evidence: No server_tokens off; or autoindex off; directives.
findings.impact: Minor information disclosure.
findings.remediation: Add server_tokens off; and autoindex off; to server block.

findings.title: Outdated python packages: requests 2.31.0, openai 1.10.0, fastapi 0.109.0
findings.description: Several Python packages are outdated. While no critical CVEs exist for exact versions, staying current reduces attack surface.
findings.evidence: requests==2.31.0, openai==1.10.0, fastapi==0.109.0.
findings.impact: Increased attack surface from outdated dependencies.
findings.remediation: Update to latest stable versions.

findings.title: Missing Strict-Transport-Security (HSTS) header
findings.description: HSTS header not present. Other security headers (X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, CSP, X-XSS-Protection, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy) are properly configured.
findings.evidence: All security headers present except Strict-Transport-Security.
findings.impact: Protocol downgrade attacks possible in production.
findings.remediation: Add Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload after enabling HTTPS.

findings.title: Admin panel login page publicly accessible at /admin
findings.description: /admin returns 302 redirect to login page. Requires authentication but is publicly discoverable. /telescope, /horizon, /_debugbar properly return 404.
findings.evidence: GET /admin -> 302 redirect to login form.
findings.impact: Admin panel discovery aids targeted attacks.
findings.remediation: Restrict /admin by IP at nginx level.

regression.finding: Public PII Exposure via Tracking Endpoints
regression.evidence: POST /api/v1/complaints/track/{trackingNumber} returns all DB columns including national_id, phone, email without auth. Print endpoint strips PII fields (partial improvement).
backend/routes/admin.php uses Route::middleware('auth') without role-based checks. Any authenticated user can access admin routes.
localStorage.getItem('auth_token') found in 60+ locations across frontend-next/src/.
Log::info("OTP for user {$user->email}: {$otp}") at AuthController.php:132 and similar in UserController.php:565, ComplaintController.php:71.
resend-2fa has throttle:3,10 middleware. But verify-2fa and forgot-password still lack throttle middleware.
POST /api/v1/webhooks/whatsapp/ with fake payload and no X-Hub-Signature-256 -> 500 (processes then crashes, not 401).
Database seeders contain hardcoded passwords for admin and test users.
Tracking numbers now use CMP-YYYYMMDD-XXXXX format with a random suffix (5 chars). Better than sequential but still partially predictable due to date prefix.
AdminIpRestrictionMiddleware allows all traffic when allowed_ips config is empty (fails open instead of closed).

regression.finding: Raw Exception Details in API Responses
regression.evidence: APP_DEBUG=true in backend/.env. 500 errors return full stack traces with 263KB HTML debug pages. Controllers also return $e->getMessage() in JSON responses.
No target="_blank" links without rel="noopener noreferrer" found in blade templates.
python-multipart 0.0.6 (CVE-2024-24762), Pillow 10.2.0 (multiple CVEs), google-genai unpinned, requests/openai/fastapi outdated.

regression.finding: Docker Exposes PostgreSQL with Static Credentials
regression.evidence: Port 5432 still exposed in both compose files. POSTGRES_PASSWORD=surian_pass unchanged.
CORS allows /^https?:\/\/.*\.gov\.sy$/ with credentials. CSRF exempts complaints/*, chat/*, suggestions/*, webhooks/*.
